title: Bender Should Not Be Allowed On Tv

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Father. But somewhere along the way, I forgot how to be your son. Why, Antonio? Because I have amnesia!
jokes.setup: Emotional father-son confrontation about losing their relationship
jokes.punchline: The reason is the soap opera cliche of amnesia
jokes.explanation: Classic soap opera parody with tight structure - the absurdly simple medical explanation undercuts the dramatic moment

jokes.text: Calculon! But I thought you were-- Egyptian?
jokes.setup: Character sees Calculon and starts dramatic recognition line
jokes.punchline: Expected 'dead' but gets random nationality
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy - subverts soap opera convention with random absurdity

jokes.text: Let me get this straight. Does anyone here not have amnesia? Not sure. I don't know.
jokes.setup: Character tries to clarify the amnesia situation
jokes.punchline: The responses themselves demonstrate amnesia
jokes.explanation: Smart recursive joke where the punchline proves the setup's premise - well-crafted logical absurdity

jokes.text: This show is awesome! When I grow up, I'm gonna have so much amnesia.
jokes.setup: Kids watching soap opera admiringly
jokes.punchline: Child treats serious medical condition like a cool aspiration
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing how kids misunderstand TV - Cubert's naive enthusiasm works

jokes.text: Me too! I mean, I have it now. But I forgot. Well, mine's louder!
jokes.setup: Kids competing over wanting amnesia
jokes.punchline: One claims to already have it but forgot, other makes it competitive
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of the amnesia joke with kid logic - the competitive aspect adds absurdity

jokes.text: Cram a ham in it, you twerps!
jokes.setup: Kids being loud and annoying
jokes.punchline: Bender's creative variation on 'cram it'
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender wordplay - creative food-based insult that's both crude and inventive

jokes.text: Cool! I'm gonna have a fabulatious birthday party just like Calculon!
jokes.setup: Kid excited about TV party
jokes.punchline: Made-up word combining 'fabulous' and probably 'delicious'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay showing kid enthusiasm - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: We're 12. So, yes.
jokes.setup: Adult asks if kids have to imitate everything on TV
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact acknowledgment that being 12 explains everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with excellent economy - captures universal truth about 12-year-olds

jokes.text: Do clones even have birthdays? Duh. Duh what?
jokes.setup: Philosophical question about clone birthdays gets dismissive 'duh'
jokes.punchline: Follow-up reveals the 'duh' person has no actual answer
jokes.explanation: Good structure exposing fake confidence - the reversal works well

jokes.text: We can celebrate the day I extracted you from the cloning tank. Or the day I scraped your DNA from that growth on my back.
jokes.setup: Professor offers alternative birthday celebrations
jokes.punchline: Second option is disgusting DNA scraping from back growth
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor-specific grossout humor with perfect escalation - the scientific matter-of-factness makes it funnier

jokes.text: That one! Yes. It was 13 years ago next week. I used this very fork.
jokes.setup: Cubert enthusiastically chooses the gross DNA scraping option
jokes.punchline: Professor provides disturbing detail about using dinner fork for medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with visual component - the casual unsanitariness is perfectly Professor-like

jokes.text: Wait! I thought of a way! The way I just said!
jokes.setup: Professor acts like he's having a breakthrough idea
jokes.punchline: He's just repeating what he literally just said
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor senility joke - his confusion about his own recent words is character-specific

jokes.text: For you see, the horrible secret is-- [scene cuts away at dramatic moment]
jokes.setup: Soap opera building to dramatic revelation
jokes.punchline: Show cuts away at the exact moment of revelation
jokes.explanation: Classic soap opera trope subversion - the interrupted revelation.

jokes.text: Whoever's directing this is a master of suspense!
jokes.setup: Dramatic cliffhanger moment in soap opera
jokes.punchline: Character praises the directing of the very show we're watching
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor that breaks the fourth wall - works but not exceptional

jokes.text: He's busteds. Let's get hims out of heres.
jokes.setup: Robot police arriving at scene
jokes.punchline: Robot speech pattern adding unnecessary 's' to words
jokes.explanation: Standard robot speech gag - functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: This show's been going downhill since season three.
jokes.setup: Watching 'Everybody Loves Hypno-Toad'
jokes.punchline: Bender criticizes a show that's literally just a hypnotic toad
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about TV criticism - the idea that a hypnotic toad show could decline in quality is absurdly perfect

jokes.text: Since when are you performing at children's parties? Performing? What? Please, if someone could spare me money to buy shoes?
jokes.setup: Leela assuming Zoidberg is hired entertainment
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg is actually just begging for money
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg moment - his poverty misinterpreted as entertainment.

jokes.text: This party pukes. Yeah! The guests were supposed to be here three hours ago.
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about their party
jokes.punchline: Reveals the sad reality that no one came
jokes.explanation: Standard sad party setup - functional but predictable cringe humor

jokes.text: Well, that doesn't mean that no one is coming or that you two are total losers. Who gave you that idea?
jokes.setup: Leela tries to be reassuring about the party
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines herself by asking who suggested they're losers
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing Leela's accidental tactlessness - the reversal works

jokes.text: Good day, fellows. Someone dumped this invitation in my begging cup.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg arrives at the party
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's only there because invitation was thrown away in his begging cup
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his poverty and social awkwardness perfectly captured in one line

jokes.text: Tinny Tim! Are you thinking what I'm thinking? What's that, sir? That I, Bender, am perfect for the role! You raised my hopes and dashed them quite expertly, sir. Bravo!
jokes.setup: Bender asks if Tim is thinking the same thing
jokes.punchline: Tim's hopes are raised then crushed when Bender's 'shared' thought is just about himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-driven misdirection - Bender's narcissism and Tim's polite disappointment work perfectly together

jokes.text: Look, there's Macaulay Culkon! He's just not cute since he got puberty installed.
jokes.setup: Pointing out robot version of famous child actor
jokes.punchline: Puberty is described as an installation like software
jokes.explanation: Clever robot-specific take on child star aging - the 'installed' concept is original

jokes.text: And there's that robot child actor who grew up and became a convenience store. Lottery ticket, please.
jokes.setup: Pointing out another former child actor
jokes.punchline: The actor literally became a building/business, still functioning as a store
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd take on child star career paths - the literalness of becoming a store is inspired

jokes.text: Now that I know the truth, Father, I must ride south and join the robo-Bandidos at Veracruz!
jokes.setup: Child robot doing dramatic audition scene
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic dialogue mixing family drama with Western robot gangs
jokes.explanation: Standard soap opera parody with robot elements - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Boo! Boo! This kid sucks! We want Bender! Boo!
jokes.setup: Child actor performing poorly
jokes.punchline: Audience demands Bender specifically despite him not auditioning yet
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag of audience demanding Bender - functional setup

jokes.text: Have you ever been on TV before? Once, when I took those hostages.
jokes.setup: Standard casting question about TV experience
jokes.punchline: Bender casually mentions criminal activity as his TV credit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - casual criminality delivered with perfect economy and character truth

jokes.text: I will see. Adiós, Padre. Come, Jesus, my faithful chihuahua. Tonight we eat guacamole by the EI Rio!
jokes.setup: Bender claims to have flawless Spanish accent
jokes.punchline: Delivers stereotypical, terrible Spanish performance with random elements
jokes.explanation: Good Bender overconfidence joke - his terrible performance after claiming expertise works

jokes.text: That was so terrible I think you gave me cancer! I don't care how popular you are, you will never work on my show! However, you've got the job.
jokes.setup: Director absolutely destroys Bender's audition
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal based on audience demand despite artistic standards
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure showing Hollywood hypocrisy - the complete 180 is perfectly timed

jokes.text: I read the script, and I think it would help my character's motivation if he was on fire.
jokes.setup: Bender offering acting insights to director
jokes.punchline: His contribution is wanting to literally be on fire
jokes.explanation: Great Bender logic - treating destructive chaos as serious character development is perfect for him

jokes.text: We rewrote your part to suit your acting abilities. So now my character has a British accent? No, now your character's in a coma.
jokes.setup: Script rewritten for Bender's 'abilities' - he guesses accent work
jokes.punchline: Actually rewritten to require zero acting - just lying unconscious
jokes.explanation: Sharp burn disguised as professional accommodation - the coma solution is brutally funny

jokes.text: Stupid dumb coma. I could've been British.
jokes.setup: Bender unhappy with coma role
jokes.punchline: His complaint focuses on missed accent opportunity rather than being unconscious
jokes.explanation: Good Bender priorities joke - focusing on trivial aspect while missing the insult

jokes.text: Hey, everyone! Antonio here, but you can call me Bender! I got ants in my butt and I needs to strut!
jokes.setup: Bender supposed to be in coma
jokes.punchline: Breaks character completely with energetic introduction and crude dancing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender inability to follow direction - crude energy opposite of what's needed

jokes.text: I'm not familiar with the type of thing I'm seeing.
jokes.setup: Bender's inappropriate dancing and behavior on set
jokes.punchline: Calculon's confused, understated reaction to chaos
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understated reaction - Calculon's dramatic personality making this so mild is perfect

jokes.text: No. No, no, no. I don't do two takes. Amateurs like you do two takes. I do one take.
jokes.setup: Director wants to reshoot scene after terrible performance
jokes.punchline: Bender acts like prima donna, claiming one take is professional superiority
jokes.explanation: Great Bender arrogance - spinning his laziness as professional standard

jokes.text: We didn't. That's security camera footage from your dressing room.
jokes.setup: Calculon compliments Bender's 'hospital dancing'
jokes.punchline: Reveals the footage is from security camera, not actual performance
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Bender's unprofessionalism - the security footage reveal is perfect

jokes.text: Executive Alpha, programmed to like things it has seen before. Hey, hey, hey. Executive Beta. Programmed to roll dice to determine the fall schedule. More reality shows!
jokes.setup: Introduction of network executives
jokes.punchline: Each exec is programmed for different TV industry cliches
jokes.explanation: Smart satire of TV industry decision-making as purely algorithmic

jokes.text: And Executive Gamma, programmed to underestimate Middle America. It's funny, but is it going to get them off their tractors?
jokes.setup: Third executive introduction
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical condescending view of rural audiences
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of TV exec satire - the tractor reference is appropriately condescending

jokes.text: At our network, we love filth. Filthy rich, that is. Being filthy rich, that is.
jokes.setup: Network defending Bender's inappropriate content
jokes.punchline: Awkward clarification that 'filth' means money, then over-clarifying
jokes.explanation: Clunky wordplay that over-explains itself - the repetition weakens it

jokes.text: 'Bite my shiny metal ass' could be a catchphrase. Eighty percent likely. It will play in Peoria. Game shows are back!
jokes.setup: Executives analyzing Bender's crude phrase
jokes.punchline: Clinical analysis of catchphrase potential with random scheduling decisions
jokes.explanation: Good TV industry satire - reducing creative decisions to market research data.

jokes.text: Look. I'm Bender!
jokes.setup: Kid imitating Bender's TV behavior
jokes.punchline: Visual of kid copying Bender's crude gestures
jokes.explanation: Basic setup for kids copying TV - functional but predictable

jokes.text: The ruffians smoked one of your cigars. That's not a cigar!
jokes.setup: Concern about kids smoking cigars
jokes.punchline: It's not actually a cigar (implying something worse)
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection - sets up parental concern then reveals it's actually worse than expected

jokes.text: Hey, Dad. Bite my shiny metal ass!
jokes.setup: Kid copying Bender's catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Using crude language to parent
jokes.explanation: Callback creates expected shock value - functional but predictable outcome

jokes.text: What? Such an act would be most uncomfortable for both of us!
jokes.setup: Professor hearing Bender's catchphrase from Cubert
jokes.punchline: Takes the crude suggestion literally and practically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor response - his scientific literalness makes the crude phrase awkwardly practical

jokes.text: From Bender, my good jerkwad.
jokes.setup: Professor asking where Cubert learned the language
jokes.punchline: Cubert adds 'jerkwad' while being polite about it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Cubert's polite rudeness - the contradiction works

jokes.text: Bender? That guy's really starting to twist my dreads.
jokes.setup: Hermes upset about Bender's influence
jokes.punchline: Jamaican-specific version of 'twist my arm' idiom
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific language - adapting common idiom to Hermes' appearance.

jokes.text: What kind of bozos would start a Bender protest group? Good news, everyone! Hermes and I have started a Bender protest group!
jokes.setup: Fry dismissively asks what kind of people would protest Bender
jokes.punchline: Professor immediately enters announcing he's done exactly that
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and structure - the immediate contradiction is expertly executed

jokes.text: That was uncanny.
jokes.setup: Perfect timing of Professor's entrance
jokes.punchline: Fry acknowledging the comedic timing
jokes.explanation: Mild meta acknowledgment of the setup - works as a beat but not exceptional

jokes.text: No, sir! Not us F.A.R.T.Ers!
jokes.setup: Group called 'Fathers Against Rude Television'
jokes.punchline: Acronym spells FART, members called FARTers
jokes.explanation: Obvious acronym joke that relies on toilet humor - functional but lazy

jokes.text: Our dads are all pumped up on dork-Osterone.
jokes.setup: Kids dismissing their fathers' concerns
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'dork' and 'testosterone'
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau showing kid perspective on adult behavior - works well

jokes.text: Our dads are all pumped up on dork-Osterone.
jokes.setup: Kids discussing their fathers' anti-TV stance
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'dork' and 'testosterone'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay - clever portmanteau that captures the kids' perspective on their fathers.

jokes.text: We could commit a burglary. Hey, yeah! Bender loves to burgle!
jokes.setup: Kids trying to emulate Bender without getting sick
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion to commit felonies, using 'burgle' as cute verb
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of TV influence theme - 'burgle' as casual verb adds levity to dark suggestion

jokes.text: Hold up, though. Who could we rob? We don't know anyone with cool stuff. Duh. We know someone who runs on pure cool fuel: Bender!
jokes.setup: Kids planning robbery but lacking targets
jokes.punchline: Deciding to rob their role model because he's cool
jokes.explanation: Good logical progression - kids' criminal plan leads to ironic target choice.

jokes.text: Whoa! We'll rob Bender! TV gave us the idea!
jokes.setup: Kids deciding who to rob
jokes.punchline: Ironically robbing their TV influence while crediting TV for the idea
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic twist - robbing their role model while crediting the medium is clever

jokes.text: Of course! You don't have friends!
jokes.setup: Professor realizing something's wrong with Cubert's story
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest observation about Cubert's social life
jokes.explanation: Good Professor tactlessness - his scientific approach to social observation works

jokes.text: Of course! You don't have friends!
jokes.setup: Professor puzzling over his sons having a party
jokes.punchline: Realizing the logical flaw - they have no friends
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment - his scientific deduction leads to brutal honesty about his sons.

jokes.text: Good Lord! I'm getting a reading of over 40 mega-Fonzies.
jokes.setup: Professor using Cool-O-Meter device
jokes.punchline: Coolness measured in units named after the Fonz
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake scientific measurement using pop culture reference - very quotable

jokes.text: Good Lord! I'm getting a reading of over 40 mega-Fonzies.
jokes.setup: Professor using Cool-O-Meter on the kids
jokes.punchline: Measuring coolness in units named after the Fonz
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference - using Fonzie as the unit of coolness measurement.

jokes.text: We stole it! Oh, crumb.
jokes.setup: Cubert dramatically declares loyalty and secrecy
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately confesses everything, Cubert's mild disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good structure with immediate betrayal of secrecy - Cubert's mild reaction adds to it

jokes.text: Hey. This is my stuff they stole!
jokes.setup: Bender defending TV's right to be irresponsible
jokes.punchline: Suddenly cares about responsibility when it affects him personally
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bender's hypocrisy when consequences affect him personally

jokes.text: Hey. This is my stuff they stole!
jokes.setup: Bender defending entertainment value over role model concerns
jokes.punchline: Suddenly caring when he realizes it's his stuff that was stolen
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his principles immediately crumble when personally affected.

jokes.text: Bender should not be allowed on television!
jokes.setup: Bender realizing his influence caused him to be robbed
jokes.punchline: Advocates for his own censorship - the episode title
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal that delivers the episode title - Bender censoring himself works

jokes.text: I once put a laugh track on a sitcom that had no jokes in it.
jokes.setup: Director threatening with his capacity for violence
jokes.punchline: His ultimate crime is laugh track abuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on TV comedy - treating it as ultimate evil is perfect

jokes.text: Have you ever tried simply turning off the TV, sitting down with your children, and hitting them?
jokes.setup: Bender giving parenting advice about TV alternatives
jokes.punchline: Suggests physical violence as bonding activity
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection with perfect structure - sets up wholesome advice then subverts completely. Became iconic quote.

jokes.text: Have you ever tried simply turning off the TV... Sitting down with your children... And hitting them?
jokes.setup: Bender building to inspirational parenting advice
jokes.punchline: Advocating child abuse as solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - sets up wholesome parenting advice then subverts to violence. Classic Futurama dark humor.

jokes.text: That we should all take TV a little less seriously. And more importantly, turn it off once in a while. So should we turn it off now? Well That depends what's on.
jokes.setup: Characters learning lesson about not watching too much TV
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradict the lesson by staying glued to TV
jokes.explanation: Standard ending irony - characters don't follow their own advice. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Nothing good. Let's just keep watching.
jokes.setup: Characters deciding whether to turn off TV after learning to watch less
jokes.punchline: Immediately defaulting back to TV watching despite message
jokes.explanation: Good ending irony - characters ignore the episode's lesson immediately.

tmdb_overview: Bender becomes a television star as well as a terrible role model for children.
